body: Stripe account approved for Shiloh Baptist Church 501(c)(3). EIN verified. Using nonprofit rate: 2.2% + $0.30 per transaction. Payment Element rendering in staging. Three categories confirmed with Deacon Pittman.
Testing in staging looks good. I would like to see the receipt email before we go live — want to make sure it says Shiloh Baptist Church, not just Stripe. Can you send a test receipt to my email?

Test receipt sent to wilkins@shilohbaptistbiloxi.org. The from-name is now 'Shiloh Baptist Church' and the email body includes the church address and EIN for tax records. Let me know once you have reviewed.
Started the sermon import. Used Squoosh to process the audio thumbnails. The Webflow API rate limit is 60 requests/minute — batching the 47 sermon posts into groups of 10. About halfway done.

The pipeline is failing on the sermon import script — missing a date field in the CSV. Some older sermons don't have a date recorded. Should we default to the WordPress post date, or mark those as undated?
Use the WordPress post date as default for any sermon without an explicit date. We can always correct later. Keep moving — getting sermons on the Webflow site is important for the launch.

Opened ticket with Webflow support (ticket #WF-2026-8814). They confirmed that forms submitted from subdomains using their default domain may experience delivery delays. Recommended adding a custom SMTP relay via Zapier or Make.com.

Hero image converted from JPEG (2.1MB) to WebP (380KB) using Squoosh CLI. Updated the Webflow CMS hero_image field. Lighthouse mobile score is now 71 — just over the 70 target. Closing this once the font-display:swap is in.
Added the alt_text custom field to the Webflow Gallery collection. Batch-updating via the Webflow CMS API. About 30 of 45 images done. Will complete the rest this week.

Great work on the gallery alt text. I reviewed a few — they look descriptive and appropriate. For images of people, make sure we use their names if they are members (e.g., 'Deacon Raymond Owens at 2025 Homecoming service').

Gloria, the Bulletins CMS collection is set up in Webflow. You have Editor access. To upload a new bulletin: go to editor.webflow.com, click Bulletins, click New Item, set the date, title, and upload the PDF. Then click Publish. Let me know if you need a walkthrough.
I tested the Bulletins upload with last Sunday's bulletin PDF. It worked perfectly. Thank you, Calvin! This will save us so much time every week.

VBS form is live on staging. Added the background check consent checkbox. Also added a note that consent is required for anyone working with children ages 12 and under. Theresa, can you review the field order?
Reviewed the VBS form — field order looks good. One addition: please add a field for 'T-shirt size' since we give all volunteers a VBS shirt. S, M, L, XL, XXL should cover it.
Got the headshot and bio from the church office for Rev. Hooks. Uploading the photo to Webflow assets now. Bio text is about 80 words — fits well with the existing staff card layout.

The Certbot dry run is now passing after updating the Cloudflare API token. The cert renewal should auto-run on the first of each month at 2 AM. I verified the cron entry: 0 2 1 * * certbot renew --quiet.
Received the 75th anniversary ZIP from Dorothy. 12 photos total — 8 JPEGs and 4 PNGs. All over 2MB. Compressing with Squoosh before uploading to Webflow. Will have the page updated by end of week.

John, the Building Committee page will need a section listing all committee members. I have your contact info as john.baker@Finthesiss.ai — is that okay to display publicly, or would you prefer the church listing?
Calvin, just list me as John Baker — no email on the page is fine with me. The church office can relay any inquiries. Thank you for all your work on the site, it is really coming together.
Fall Revival dates confirmed by Pastor Wilkins: September 14-17, 2026. Guest evangelist will be announced in August. Added to The Events Calendar plugin. All 2026 events are now in the calendar.
